sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund 12 months of Eternl operations, maintenance, support, infrastructure, audits, and continued development while introducing paid Pro plans.

Deliverables: 12 months of frontend and backend maintenance and development, backend infrastructure and operations, user support and day-to-day operations, public company wallet for monitoring converted stablecoins, periodic independent audits of treasury fund use in February 2027 and August 2027, on-chain metadata records for paid Pro plans and published reports on Pro plan earnings, treasury repayment or donation transactions if stated surplus/income thresholds are met
Deadline/expiry: Delivery period August 2026 to July 2027

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Eternl requests ₳2,350,000, valued at about $0.1787 per Ada, to cover approximately $420,000 of annual operating cost for August 2026 through July 2027.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposed budget allocates 55% to frontend, 25% to backend, 5% to support, 13% to admin, and 2% to audits.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Eternl says its work is performed by 10 contributors comprising 6.0 FTE at $70,000 per FTE.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): Eternl plans to introduce Pro plans priced at $96 per year for Personal users and $360 per year for Company users.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Eternl claims that about 4.2% of its install base, roughly 5,500 users, buying Pro plans would fully cover its annual $420,000 cost.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Eternl states it has about 100,000 browser-extension installs and about 30,000 installs across Android and iOS.
missing: Independent evidence for: Eternl says its work is performed by 10 contributors comprising 6.0 FTE at $70,000 per FTE.
Independent evidence for: Eternl states it has about 100,000 browser-extension installs and about 30,000 installs across Android and iOS.
Independent evidence for: Eternl states it will convert up to $420,000 worth of Ada into stablecoins in a public company wallet and return any Ada above that amount to the treasury.

Independent evidence for: Eternl states an independent unaffiliated party will audit treasury fund use in February 2027 and August 2027, covering receipt, custody, conversion, spending, balances, Pro plan income relevant to repayment, and treasury refunds or repayments.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Eternl requests ₳2,350,000, valued at about $0.1787 per Ada, to cover approximately $420,000 of annual operating cost for August 2026 through July 2027." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Eternl says its work is performed by 10 contributors comprising 6.0 FTE at $70,000 per FTE." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
